itemRefs: Severed head of the shaggy white beast
sourceShortSummaries: The party lands in the black-stone pit, the shaggy white beast calls up Living Snow echoes around them, and the fight begins in confusion.
As the fight settles into a brutal middle phase, the party protects Kalima and experiments with spells and summons to crack the Living Snow's defenses.
The party finally decodes the echoes' pattern, clears most of them, and swings the battle back toward the beast.
With the last echoes gone, the party batters the exposed beast until Eolo's psionic volley kills it and the chamber changes.
After the beast dies, the party takes a trophy, learns three truths about it, and follows Kalima through the new earthen tunnel to a cliffside overlook above Melusa.
Kalima hustles the party up the switchbacks and quietly into Melusa, where the village's five gashes and looming ice wolf show that something has changed.
sourceLongSummaries: Fresh from the slide into the chamber, the party gets its first clear look at the shaggy white beast and the strange Living Snow echoes it raises from the icy ground around each hero. The beat is about surviving the opening scramble, realizing the echoes can slow and harry them, and keeping Kalima hidden behind Finnan's illusion.

The party starts testing what can actually hurt the Living Snow and the beast they protect, with Ekko's conjured pack, Justas's Spirit Guardians, and Finnan's fey spirit revealing that some attacks land while others slide away or stiffen harmlessly against the snow. Kalima remains concealed, and the group's immediate goal becomes keeping her alive while understanding the encounter's rules.

Comparing notes, the party realizes the Living Snow echoes resemble the people they first appeared beside, and they begin cross-targeting them with the attacks that actually work. Spirit Guardians, Rala's strikes, Eolo's shots, and Finnan's psychic magic collapse the remaining echoes and open the beast to direct pressure.

Once the final Living Snow collapses, Ekko and Justas land real damage on the beast and its own attacks start looking desperate rather than dominant. Eolo's final psionic shot drops it, the wind stops, and the chamber begins melting back from ice to granite and earth.

The chamber sheds its frost and opens a dirt-scented tunnel, but the party delays just long enough for Finnan to pull three facts from the beast and for Justas to hack off its head as a trophy. They crawl through the permafrost passage, emerge onto a narrow switchback above the Great Chasm, and send Finnan's fey spirit toward the Ice Palace to seek an audience with Forquarion the Frostbinder.

From the cliffside path the party gets its first clear look at Melusa, the waterfall-fed pond at its center, and the floating rocks around the Great Chasm. Kalima insists they stay hidden, points out that five jagged gashes have appeared in the cliff face beneath a huge ice wolf statue, and leads them into her sister Nura and brother-in-law Khaled's house.

sourceEntries.title: Ambush in the Pit
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The session opens with the party sliding into the arena chamber, the beast calling up living snow around each hero, and the group scrambling through the first exchange while they identify the encounter and try to stabilize their positions.

The beat is about surviving the opening scramble, realizing the echoes can slow and harry them, and keeping Kalima hidden behind Finnan's illusion.

locationHints.notes: The beat is the transition out of Zeyfa's Labyrinth: the party leaves the black-stone chamber, crawls through the new earthen passage, and emerges onto the lower cliffside approach that belongs to Melusa.

npcDetails.notes: Recognizes the tunnel as a possible way out and hurries the party through it.

npcDetails.name: Forquarion the Frostbinder

itemDetails.name: Severed head of the shaggy white beast
itemDetails.notes: Justas takes the head as a trophy instead of skinning the carcass.

sourceEntries.title: Hidden Into Melusa
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Kalima rushes the group up the switchbacks, warns them to stay unseen, and leads them through the eerie ledge village into Nura and Khalid's house, where the surviving residents receive them as strange but potentially sacred outsiders.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Kalima hustles the party up the switchbacks and quietly into Melusa, where the village's five gashes and looming ice wolf show that something has changed.
sourceEntries.longSummary: From the cliffside path the party gets its first clear look at Melusa, the waterfall-fed pond at its center, and the floating rocks around the Great Chasm. Kalima insists they stay hidden, points out that five jagged gashes have appeared in the cliff face beneath a huge ice wolf statue, and leads them into her sister Nura and brother-in-law Khaled's house.

locationHints.context: lower cliffside paths, ledge village, and Kalima's family house
locationHints.notes: The beat covers movement through sublocations within Melusa rather than travel between separate named places.

npcDetails.name: Kalima
npcDetails.notes: Leads the party home, warns them to stay hidden, and introduces them to her family.

npcDetails.name: Nura
npcDetails.notes: Kalima's sister, shocked but willing to shelter the party.

npcDetails.name: Khaled
npcDetails.notes: Kalima's brother-in-law, initially protective of Nura and Samir when strangers enter.

npcDetails.name: Samir
npcDetails.notes: Nura and Khaled's baby, present when the party first enters the house.

itemDetails.name: Light-enchanted stone
itemDetails.notes: Kalima presents the stone Justas enchanted with light as proof that the Kestavo are real.

sourceShortSummaries: Inside Nura and Khaled's house, the party learns how Melusa understands Kestavo, endurance, death, sunset, and the village's isolation.
sourceLongSummaries: Conversation in the safe house turns into a crash course on Melusa's worldview: the villagers half-remember the Kestavo as stories, think the outside world is barely real, and explain survival as simple endurance inside the fog. Nura, Khaled, Kalima, and baby Samir together reveal a community shaped by sacrifice, strange ideas of death and memory, and a sleep cycle ruled by the Ice Palace's gong.

sourceEntries.title: House of Endurance
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Inside the safe house, the party learns how Melusa understands Kestavo, sacrifice, sunset, and survival, while the villagers reveal just enough of their cosmology and fears for the players to realize how deeply isolated the settlement has become.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Inside Nura and Khaled's house, the party learns how Melusa understands Kestavo, endurance, death, sunset, and the village's isolation.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Conversation in the safe house turns into a crash course on Melusa's worldview: the villagers half-remember the Kestavo as stories, think the outside world is barely real, and explain survival as simple endurance inside the fog. Nura, Khaled, Kalima, and baby Samir together reveal a community shaped by sacrifice, strange ideas of death and memory, and a sleep cycle ruled by the Ice Palace's gong.

locationHints.notes: The party remains inside the family house while they talk, eat, and question their hosts about Melusa and the world beyond it.

npcDetails.name: Nura
npcDetails.notes: Speaks about family, death, and the fear that children can remember too much.

npcDetails.name: Khaled
npcDetails.notes: Explains Melusa's ethic of endurance and steers the conversation away from dangerous questions.

npcDetails.name: Kalima
npcDetails.notes: Adds details about recent sunset and helps bridge the party's questions with her family's worldview.

npcDetails.name: Samir
npcDetails.notes: Unusually quiet baby whose soul may 'remember too much,' according to Nura.

organizationDetails.name: Kestavo
organizationDetails.notes: The conversation focuses on Melusa's half-remembered stories of Kestavo and the villagers' belief that no Kestavo are present there.

sourceShortSummaries: After the gong rings, Ekko scouts Melusa's gashes while Finnan uses Clairvoyance on the Ice Palace, revealing guarded portals and an ice bridge growing toward the village.
sourceLongSummaries: Night in Melusa becomes a reconnaissance beat. Ekko, prowling as a gray cat, confirms the quiet village ecology, the five cliff gashes, the mirror-gash guarded by human soldiers and a slowly moving ice wolf statue, and the hostile realms visible beyond the other openings; meanwhile Finnan's Clairvoyance finds a Sylvan-inscribed disk in the palace tower and then soldiers below preparing to hunt a 'trickster' spirit as an ice bridge extends toward the village. The party chooses to hide in the barn and rest rather than act immediately.

sourceEntries.title: Portals and Night Watch
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Once the gong rings, the session turns into layered reconnaissance: Ekko scouts the village and the guarded mirror-gash, Finnan uses clairvoyance to inspect the palace and the forming ice bridge, and the party ends by choosing to hide, rest, and face the consequences tomorrow.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: After the gong rings, Ekko scouts Melusa's gashes while Finnan uses Clairvoyance on the Ice Palace, revealing guarded portals and an ice bridge growing toward the village.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Night in Melusa becomes a reconnaissance beat. Ekko, prowling as a gray cat, confirms the quiet village ecology, the five cliff gashes, the mirror-gash guarded by human soldiers and a slowly moving ice wolf statue, and the hostile realms visible beyond the other openings; meanwhile Finnan's Clairvoyance finds a Sylvan-inscribed disk in the palace tower and then soldiers below preparing to hunt a 'trickster' spirit as an ice bridge extends toward the village. The party chooses to hide in the barn and rest rather than act immediately.

locationHints.context: Nura and Khaled's house, Melusa's ledge village and cliff face, and remote scouting of the Ice Palace
locationHints.notes: Primary on-stage action remains based in Melusa, including the house, the village clusters, and the cliff face with the gashes, while Ekko scouts locally and Finnan observes the palace remotely.

npcDetails.name: human guards
npcDetails.notes: Ekko sees four human guards posted at the mirror-like gash near the ice wolf statue and guard wisps.

npcDetails.name: Ice Palace soldiers
npcDetails.notes: Finnan sees armed soldiers in the Ice Palace as the ice bridge grows toward Melusa and they discuss hunting a trickster spirit.

itemDetails.name: Sylvan-inscribed disk
itemDetails.notes: Seen via Clairvoyance in the Ice Palace tower as a circular surface marked with dense Sylvan writing and a glowing point of light.

locationHints.notes: Primary on-stage action remains based in Melusa, including the house, the village clusters, and the cliff face with the gashes, while Ekko scouts locally and Finnan observes the palace remotely.

npcDetails.name: human guards
npcDetails.notes: Ekko sees four human guards posted at the mirror-like gash near the ice wolf statue and guard wisps.

npcDetails.name: Ice Palace soldiers
npcDetails.notes: Finnan sees armed soldiers in the Ice Palace as the ice bridge grows toward Melusa and they discuss hunting a trickster spirit.

itemDetails.name: Sylvan-inscribed disk
itemDetails.notes: Seen via Clairvoyance in the Ice Palace tower as a circular surface marked with dense Sylvan writing and a glowing point of light.

locations.name: Melusa
locations.contextHint: through the earthen tunnel opened in the chamber floor and out onto Melusa's lower cliffside paths

items.name: Severed head of the shaggy white beast

npcs.name: Forquarion the Frostbinder
npcs.contextHint: master of the beast and the Ice Palace
